mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over 6 months indicates rising production capacity, leading to increased supply and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the same period, signals a strong economy and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strong economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over 3 months, combined with a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary cycle and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the number of payroll employees (PAYEMS) over 3 months signals a strong labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strong labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the number of housing starts (HOUST) over 6 months signals a weakening housing market, leading to decreased consumer spending and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
